extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50): IF (renal_impairment AND asthma AND immunosuppressed). rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50): IF renal_impairment. No rule ever assigns false to either output; the only source of false is the default, which applies solely to outputs left unset. With renal_impairment=true, asthma=true, immunosuppressed=true, both rec_a_from_conjunction and rec_b_from_trigger fire, setting rec_drug_a:=true and rec_drug_b:=true. Since both outputs are set, the default does not apply, and there is no overriding rule. Thus both are simultaneously true, violating mutual exclusion. The other inputs (heart_failure, elderly, prior_gi_bleed) only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant; set to false for a minimal witness.
